summary.intent: Audit and retrofit the existing Leptos and Dioxus Field, Fieldset, and Form adapter components against the newer 'Checkbox-era' adapter delivery workflow standards, fixing all spec drift, semantic-boundary drift, missing tests, stale examples, and parity gaps in a single PR.
summary.outcome: Successfully completed the full retrofit on branch `codex/field-form-adapter-retrofit-audit`: shared native validation logic extracted to `ars-forms`, all compound parts made publicly stylable (including `Form::StatusRegion`), root primitives renamed to the `Root` naming convention, docs/skills updated with new workflow guidance, and all local gates passed before committing and pushing a PR linked to new audit issue #730.

repo: The Checkbox component is the designated gold-standard reference for adapter implementation patterns; new and retrofitted components are measured against it.

Adapter primitives follow a module-based compound part API: module name is the component family, `Root` is the low-level root part (e.g., `field::Root`, `fieldset::Root`, `form::Root`); semantic names like `Form` are reserved for future higher-level wrappers.
Leptos adapter uses an `apply_part_attrs` helper for merging consumer `class`/`style` onto parts; Dioxus uses `props(extends = GlobalAttributes)` for the same purpose.

Shared validation semantics (native validity classification, error-map merging) belong in `ars-forms::validation`, not in adapter crates; adapters should only contain browser DOM extraction and event wiring.
`cargo xtask lint adapter-parity` validates cross-adapter API consistency; `cargo xtask spec validate` validates spec correctness; both must pass before PR.
The `xtask/src/lint.rs` composition-test heuristic uses text pattern matching to detect root component usage in composition tests and must be updated whenever public primitive names change.
The adapter delivery workflow is split across `docs/implementation/adapter-components/` (numbered files 01–13 plus new 14) with required reading order enforced by the delivery doc.
Dioxus `FormContext.reset_generation` is consumed by the Checkbox input adapter for form-reset behavior, so it cannot be removed even when it appears unused in isolated Field/Form tests.

code.finding: Dioxus `Form::StatusRegion` fallback suppression cannot rely on a child side-effect registration because children render after the parent on first SSR pass; instead the parent must inspect the child `Element` VNode tree (via `dioxus_core::DynamicNode`) before emitting the fallback.

New shared module `ars_forms::validation::native` provides `NativeValidity`, `NativeInputType`, and `merge_error_map`; these replace duplicated classifier functions that previously lived independently in both adapter `form.rs` files.

Dioxus `GlobalAttributes`-extending closures for attr merge cannot be replaced with direct method items when the callback has lifetime-bound generic parameters; scoped `#[expect(clippy::redundant_closure_for_method_calls)]` annotations are the correct fix.

The `has_composition_test` heuristic used loose text matching for `Fieldset` and `Form`; after the Root rename it required explicit patterns for `fieldset::Root` and `form::Root` to avoid false-negative adapter-parity failures.

Leptos test files that use both the core `form` spec module (for `ValidationBehavior`) and the adapter `form` module (for `form::Root`) require an alias (`use ars_components::utility::form as form_spec`) to resolve the naming collision.

workflow: TDD discipline: write a focused failing SSR/unit test before touching implementation; confirm it fails for the expected reason before adding the fix.
Concurrent browser wasm test runs cause Chrome timeouts; run adapter browser suites sequentially rather than in parallel.

Dioxus child components cannot update parent context/state before the parent renders on first SSR; fallback suppression must use pre-render information (props, VNode inspection) not runtime side effects.

Public primitive renames require a coordinated sweep: exported function names, `Props` struct names, module re-exports in preludes, all test/example/fixture call sites, spec code snippets, and xtask lint heuristics must all be updated atomically.

E2E evidence gaps should be explicitly classified: 'component assertion failed', 'harness setup failed before reaching component', or 'evidence missing because harness is blocked'; the third category keeps parity partial, not passing.
Platform capability should be recorded in spec/sketch: TypedWebDom (wasm with web_sys), WebViewBridge (Dioxus desktop via eval), ServerOrSsr (no DOM), NoDomNative (no browser semantics).

friction: E2E utility harness timed out at the 'Utility tab discovery' step (15 s timeout) on both sequential and concurrent runs, blocking Field/Form browser assertion evidence entirely; root cause was not diagnosed.

Dioxus `StatusRegion` fallback suppression required two failed approaches (child side-effect registration, then context signal) before landing on VNode tree inspection, which required reading Dioxus internal `DynamicNode` API not surfaced in project skill docs.
The `cargo xclippy` redundant-closure lint conflicted with lifetime-bound generic callbacks in Dioxus part-attr wiring; method items cannot satisfy the bound, forcing `#[expect]` annotations.

The `reset_generation` dead-code warning from wasm-only builds required a narrowly scoped `#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]` expect attribute because the field is used in native builds by the Checkbox adapter.
Perl one-liner call-site rewrite for the `Root` rename left import lines stale; a separate mechanical pass was needed to fix module aliases and remove now-nonexistent direct imports.
Browser wasm test runner failed with 'cannot spawn browser server' inside sandbox on first attempt; required a second escalated run, adding latency to an already slow wasm compile cycle.
open_items: E2E utility harness tab-discovery timeout is unresolved; Field/Form E2E browser assertion evidence is missing from the parity matrix until the harness startup blocker is fixed.
`cargo xci-fast` was not run (requires explicit user approval before push/pre-push flow); full CI gate is pending.

Dioxus desktop/native target has no typed equivalent of `web_sys` constraint validation; a future JS eval bridge (`dioxus_document::eval`) would be needed for native-target parity with browser native validity, but was intentionally deferred.
The retrofit audit identified that old examples may still model 'consumer applications' rather than pure primitive showcases; a full example audit was not completed as part of this PR.
The `Form::StatusRegion` VNode inspection approach (reading `DynamicNode` children to detect explicit part) is an internal Dioxus API; this should be revisited if Dioxus 0.8 changes the VNode shape.
